responseMessageContents.text:
I've cloned CownIt to iOS as a native Swift/SwiftUI app, mirroring the Android version's functionality and flat professional design.

What's built in `ios-cownit/`:
- **Top bar** — ink-black checkmark logo + "Cown**It**" wordmark on the left, three-button segmented role switcher (Individual / HR / CEO, defaults to Individual) + notifications button on the right.
- **Role-aware sidebar** — compact 76pt icon rail whose items reflow per role, with the emerald-soft selected state.
- **Dashboard content** — KPI stat cards with trend deltas, flat cards, status badges, animated progress bars, initials avatars, and per-role sections (commitments/check-ins, departments/needs-attention, objective alignment/performance).
- **Design system** — matching emerald accent, ink text, hairline 0.5px borders, white surfaces, no gradients/shadows, and a tuned type scale. Generated a matching app icon (emerald checkmark on ink squircle).

One thing to flag: the iOS build check couldn't run — **building/previewing native iOS apps requires a Rork Max plan**. The code is complete and written to compile, but you'll need to upgrade to Rork Max to build and preview it in the iOS simulator. The Android app is unaffected.

Want me to add anything iOS-specific next (e.g. tab bar layout for smaller screens, or splitting the rail into a proper `NavigationSplitView` for iPad)?

responseMessageContents.title: Created the iOS version of the CownIt app
responseMessageContents.description: We built a new version of the app for iPhone and iPad that matches the professional design and features of the original version, including the ability to switch between Individual, HR, and CEO views.
